methods.description: Simulations of the Last Glacial Maximum (LGM) using global climate models MPI-ESM-P and AWI-ESM, dynamically downscaled with the Weather Research and Forecasting model (WRF) to study climate conditions.

steps.description: LGM simulations of two global climate models, namely MPI-ESM-P and AWI-ESM, are dynamically downscaled with the Weather Research and Forecasting model (WRF).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform LGM simulations using MPI-ESM-P and AWI-ESM models, downscale with WRF

steps.description: The atmospheric boundary conditions are updated every 6 h, and sea surface temperature (SST) and sea ice cover are updated daily.
steps.searchable_summary: Update atmospheric boundary conditions and SST/sea ice cover

steps.description: The coastlines, ice sheet extent, trace gas conditions, and orbital parameters are adapted to LGM values according to the PMIP3 protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Adapt model parameters to LGM values using PMIP3 protocol

steps.description: The first model domain covers large parts of Europe with a horizontal resolution of 50 km and 35 vertical layers up to 150 hPa.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up model domain for Europe with specified resolution and layers

steps.description: The second, nested domain covers southern parts of the FIS, the Alps, and France with a horizontal resolution of 12.5 km.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up nested model domain for FIS, Alps, and France

methods.description: Derivation of permafrost distribution from climate model data using multiple methods to assess permafrost limits during the LGM.

steps.description: The permafrost distribution is derived from climate model data using the three different methods described in Sect. 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Derive permafrost distribution using climate model data

steps.description: For MAAT, the 2 m air temperature is considered. Threshold values were derived from data compiled from studies in current Arctic regions.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze MAAT using 2 m air temperature and Arctic data

steps.description: The surface frost index (SFI) is based on the annual freezing and thawing degree-days (DDF and DDT respectively).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate SFI using DDF and DDT

steps.description: With the direct method, permafrost is inferred when ground temperatures are at or below 0 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Infer permafrost using ground temperature data

steps.description: To evaluate the model simulations, the distribution of ice-wedge pseudomorphs and sand wedges after Andrieux et al. (2016b) and Isarin et al. (1998) are considered.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate model simulations using ice-wedge and sand wedge data
